attributes.description: Alerts when the scheduler observes any tasks killed externally (`airflow.scheduler.tasks.killed_externally > 0`). External kills usually come from OOM killers, pod evictions, or SIGKILL from autoscalers.
attributes.name: [Airflow OTel] Tasks killed externally

investigation_guide.blob:
## Airflow tasks killed externally

### What fired
The counter `airflow.scheduler.tasks.killed_externally` increased during the evaluation window.

### Why it matters
Airflow marks a task as externally killed when its state transitions unexpectedly (not by the task itself or the scheduler). The usual cause is infrastructure: the Linux OOM killer, Kubernetes pod evictions, spot/preemptible VM reclamation, or container runtime kills.

### Triage
1. Tasks dashboard: identify which tasks and when they were killed.
2. Check host/pod memory and CPU metrics at the kill time.
3. Review cluster autoscaler / spot reclamation events.
4. Look at task logs for sudden truncation.

### Remediation
- Raise worker resource requests / limits.
- Move sensitive tasks off preemptible infrastructure.
- Add checkpointing to long-running tasks so they can resume after a kill.

### Tuning
- Fires on any non-zero value. Raise the threshold only if occasional kills are part of steady-state operation.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-airflow.otel-default*
| WHERE data_stream.dataset == "airflow.otel"
| WHERE airflow.scheduler.tasks.killed_externally IS NOT NULL
// Counter: tasks killed by OOM, node eviction, etc.
| STATS killed_count = SUM(airflow.scheduler.tasks.killed_externally)
| WHERE killed_count > 0
